title: lippia longipedunculata
intro: Lippia longipedunculata, also known as wild basil or long-stalked lippia, is a plant species within the Lippia genus of the verbena family, characterized by its distinctive leaves and purple flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Lippia longipedunculata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches and body aches, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties to combat infections. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
